reviewer: solo new-family build 2026-07-21: fine-grained bird-species recognition proven FIRST in headless Chrome (transformers.js image-classification WASM fp32) on clearly-licensed Wikimedia photos, then the deployed page driven end-to-end. Sample photos are CC0/CC-BY/CC-BY-SA Wikimedia Commons images, downscaled + stored locally + ledgered + credited.

rubric.evidence: Correctness verified in headless Chrome (transformers.js image-classification, WASM fp32) BEFORE building. On clearly-licensed Wikimedia bird photos the EfficientNet-B2 names the species with high confidence: American Flamingo 1.00, Bald Eagle 1.00, Peacock 1.00, Atlantic Puffin 1.00. Deployed page (scripts/validate-bird-species-classification.mjs 10/10) reproduces these via the real pipeline with a top-5 ranking. The guesses are the model's real softmax over 525 species, not canned.
rubric.notes: DISTINCT from the built general classifiers: ImageNet ViT/ResNet/ConvNeXt/BEiT have only ~50 coarse bird classes; this is 525 FINE-GRAINED species, like food-classification is fine-grained vs ImageNet.

rubric.evidence: Input an RGB photo -> softmax over 525 bird species. A distinct capability from the built coarse classifiers: it distinguishes individual species (e.g. Atlantic Puffin vs King Eider), not just 'bird'.
rubric.notes: The 525 labels are ALL-CAPS species names; the page title-cases them for display.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the model (dennisjooo/Birds-Classifier-EfficientNetB2, linked), task (image-classification), classes (525 species), architecture (EfficientNet-B2), quantisation (fp32), backend (WASM), download (~34 MB) and license (Apache-2.0). The How-it-works block shows the real pipeline call. No invented API.
rubric.notes: transformers.js is loaded via the shared lib/webai.js loadPipeline; the 525 labels come from the model config.

rubric.evidence: Loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'dennisjooo/Birds-Classifier-EfficientNetB2', runtime:'transformers.js', dtype:'fp32', sizeMB:34}). First visit shows an explicit Download (~34 MB); the ONNX is cached by transformers.js so a returning visit auto-initialises (checking -> ready) with NO Download click. Verified: fresh profile showed Download; clicking it enabled the controls.
rubric.notes: Small model, so re-download is cheap, but the never-silently-download guarantee + auto-init on return still hold via the shared loader.

rubric.evidence: checking -> Download (~34 MB) -> ready (controls enabled) -> looking (guess placeholder) -> species + bars. Classification failures surface on the page in a role=status .err region, not only the console. Verified download -> ready -> classify end-to-end.
rubric.notes: The preview image shows what was classified; the guess line is role=status aria-live.

rubric.evidence: Drag-and-drop, a Choose-photo picker, and four clickable sample-bird thumbnails. Every control is labelled and keyboard-operable; the drop zone gives drag feedback; no dead controls.
rubric.notes: Drop rejects non-image files with a readable message; the sample thumbnails carry alt text + titles.

rubric.evidence: Every guess + bar is the model's real softmax; the readout shows the live backend + latency. The page is HONEST about limits: strongest on a clear centred bird, look-alikes/odd angles can fool it, and it will confidently pick the nearest of its 525 classes for an unknown bird - the confidence bars make a not-sure spread visible.
rubric.notes: Sample photos are real clearly-licensed images, not staged; nothing is hard-coded.

rubric.evidence: The guess is role=status aria-live; the top-5 are text bars (species name + %), so colour is never the only signal; every button has an accessible name; sample thumbnails + preview carry alt text. The shared image-credit annotator adds visible attribution for the licensed sample photos.
rubric.notes: The species name is always spelled out as text next to its bar; the bar colour is decorative.

rubric.evidence: The drop zone, thumbnails, preview and bars all wrap; the preview + results sit side-by-side on wide screens and stack on narrow ones (flex-wrap); the preview image is capped. Verified 0 horizontal overflow at desktop AND mobile 360px (validate 10/10), 0 console errors.
rubric.notes: The confidence-bar grid (name / track / %) stays legible on mobile.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs in a dedicated module Worker; a ~34 MB EfficientNet-B2 forward pass is fast (tens to low-hundreds of ms) on WASM, so classification feels instant. The download is small + one-time (cached).
rubric.notes: Image decode + resize happen in the pipeline; the UI never blocks.

guidanceConsulted.query: drag and drop image upload with an accessible file picker and sample thumbnails
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Offer accessible input paths (a real file input + drag-drop), give drag feedback, and provide zero-setup samples.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — a labelled <input type=file> + Choose-photo button + a drop zone with drag feedback + four clickable alt-texted sample thumbnails.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: upload + drop + samples all work; drop rejects non-images

guidanceConsulted.query: run image classification off the main thread and keep the UI responsive
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Do inference off the main thread; keep interactions responsive.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — classification runs in a module Worker; the fast small model keeps results near-instant.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: UI stays responsive; classification feels instant

guidanceConsulted.query: show attribution for licensed images and keep results colour-independent
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Display recorded attribution for CC-BY images, announce results in aria-live, and never rely on colour alone.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the shared image-credit annotator adds visible attribution to the licensed sample photos; the guess is aria-live; each species bar shows its name as text.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: licensed samples are credited; results readable as text

openQuestions: Would a top-1 threshold ('not sure — could be X or Y') help users when the model is between two look-alikes?
Is there a cleanly-licensed audio bird model (birdsong ID) that could pair with this for a see-and-hear field guide?
followUpGoals: Add a small map of the top species' typical range (from an open dataset) as context.
Offer a webcam mode for live identification.
Add an 'uncertain' banner when the top-1 confidence is low or the top-2 are close.
summary: Bird species classification (EfficientNet-B2, 525 species) — a distinct vision family: fine-grained species recognition (name the exact bird), vs the built general classifiers that have only ~50 coarse bird classes (like food-classification is fine-grained vs ImageNet). Proven correct in the browser on transformers.js WASM fp32 (flamingo/eagle/peacock/puffin all 1.00 on clearly-licensed Wikimedia photos). ~34 MB, Apache-2.0. Drop/choose/sample; top-5 confidence bars; licensed samples credited via the shared annotator; index demo complete + verified 10/10. Honest that look-alikes/odd angles fool it and it always picks the nearest of 525 classes. A webcam mode, range map, and an 'uncertain' banner are recorded follow-ups.
